title: Discourse markers
explanation:
Discourse markers

Discourse markers help organise ideas and show relationships between them: addition, contrast, result, example and sequencing.

  • addition: furthermore, in addition
  • contrast: however, although, nevertheless
  • result: therefore, as a result
  • example: for instance

questions.text: The train was delayed; [*however|therefore|for example], we still arrived on time.
She speaks several languages. [*In addition|Although|As a result], she has excellent communication skills.
The roads were icy. [*As a result|For instance|Nevertheless], many schools closed.
Some jobs are flexible. [*For example|However|Therefore], remote teaching and freelance design.
He was tired; [*nevertheless|for instance|because of], he finished the report.
questions.feedback: Nevertheless contrasts with the first clause.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: I enjoy outdoor sports. ____, I go hiking whenever I can. | *For instance | However | Therefore
The phone is expensive. ____, it has excellent reviews. | *However | As a result | For example
Traffic was terrible. ____, we missed the start of the film. | *As a result | In addition | Nevertheless
The company offers good pay. ____, it provides flexible hours. | *In addition | However | Therefore
The city is expensive. [*On the other hand|As a result|For example], salaries are usually higher.
He had prepared carefully; [*therefore|however|for instance], the interview went well.
[*Although|For example|In addition] she was tired, she stayed until the end.
Many people enjoy podcasts; [*for instance|however|therefore], language learners often use them for extra practice.
questions.feedback: For instance fits before an example.

questions.text: The flat is small. [*Nevertheless|For example|As a result], it feels bright and comfortable.
